payload.title: KB entries need first-class lookup aliases and symptom indexes
payload.summary: During CPY production debugging, the incident was recorded in a KB page, but future discoverability depended on whether the page happened to contain the operator phrase likely to be searched later: parquet desync from class sessions on sonar.cloud. We had to add lookup aliases manually so future searches would find the runbook.
evidence: The KB was findable for stale, class sessions, R2, and sonar.cloud, but not for the operator phrase parquet desync until a Lookup Aliases section was added.
The useful future entry point is often a symptom phrase, not the canonical system name or implementation term.
proposal: Add first-class KB metadata fields such as lookup_aliases, symptoms, systems, failure_modes, and related_runbooks.
Teach Site search and narada kb find to index lookup aliases and symptoms, not only body prose.
Add CLI affordances such as narada kb add-alias --file <path> --alias <text> and narada kb find <query>.
Incident or repair KB closure should require asking: what would the Operator or future agent search for next time?
Add a lint/check that flags incident runbooks without lookup aliases or symptom phrases.
payload.recommendation: Treat this as a cross-Site KB ergonomics improvement; CPY should be used as the concrete motivating case.
